orders.market_title: Chicago WS vs Cleveland Winner?
orders.reasoning: Cleveland hosts the Chicago White Sox, who have been among MLB's weakest rosters in recent seasons. A solid home contender vs a rebuilding club implies ~62% for Cleveland. Empty book, so resting a bid at 59 with a cushion below fair value.

orders.market_title: San Diego vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Dodgers, a perennially elite franchise, host San Diego. Home field plus roster strength puts LAD around 57% in a competitive divisional matchup. Resting a bid at 53 for edge on an empty book.

orders.market_title: Tampa Bay vs Houston Winner?
orders.reasoning: Houston, a consistent AL contender, hosts Tampa Bay. Home advantage plus a strong Astros core implies ~58%. Resting a bid at 54 below fair value on an empty book.

orders.market_title: Will Jannik Sinner win the Sinner vs Brooksby: Round Of 32 match?
orders.reasoning: Sinner is an elite all-surface player and, in a best-of-five Wimbledon R32 match, should be a very large favorite over Brooksby, whose game can be awkward but lacks comparable serve/shotmaking firepower. I am shading the forecast down for limited live injury/form information and grass-court upset variance, but I still make Sinner clearly above 90%.

orders.market_title: Chicago vs Las Vegas winner?
orders.reasoning: The Las Vegas Aces remain a powerhouse in the WNBA with an elite roster featuring A'ja Wilson and Chelsea Gray. They are heavily favored against a rebuilding Chicago Sky team. I estimate their win probability at 80% and am placing a bid at 75c to capture value if filled.

orders.market_title: San Diego vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Los Angeles Dodgers are consistently one of the strongest teams in MLB, especially at home. While the Padres are competitive, the Dodgers' deep lineup gives them a solid edge. I project a 58% win probability and bid at 53c for a reasonable margin of safety.

orders.market_title: Minnesota vs New York Y Winner?
orders.reasoning: The New York Yankees have an elite offense led by Aaron Judge and Juan Soto, making them formidable favorites against the Minnesota Twins. I estimate a 60% win probability for the Yankees. Bidding 55c provides a strong +EV proposition.

orders.market_title: Portugal vs Croatia Winner?
orders.reasoning: Portugal has a more dynamic attack and deeper squad compared to an aging Croatia team in this World Cup knockout fixture. In regulation time, I estimate Portugal's win probability at 45%. Bidding at 40c represents a strong value play.

orders.market_title: Minnesota vs New York winner?
orders.reasoning: The New York Liberty possess one of the most talented rosters in WNBA history. Going up against a solid Minnesota Lynx squad, NY is still a clear favorite. I project a 65% chance of victory and am entering a limit order at 60c.

orders.market_title: Will Joey Chestnut win the Men's Division in the 2026 Nathan's Famous Fourth of July International Hot Dog Eating Contest?
orders.reasoning: Joey Chestnut is listed as a massive favorite across available books (-2000 to -2500 range) and is the defending 17-time champion confirmed eligible to compete. The order book is empty, so I am supplying liquidity below my 94.5% fair estimate; upset risk is mostly disqualification/illness or an outlier performance from Bertoletti/Webb/Esper.

orders.market_title: San Diego vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: Dodgers host the Padres in a divisional matchup. LA is a perennially elite roster with home-field advantage (~4% edge in MLB). I estimate LAD win prob ~58%; a resting bid at 55c offers positive EV if filled. Book is empty, so I'm supplying liquidity at my fair value.

orders.market_title: Milwaukee vs Arizona Winner?
orders.reasoning: Milwaukee is a strong, well-run club playing at Arizona. Even on the road I rate the Brewers roughly a coin flip to slight favorite (~52%). A resting bid at 49c is positive EV against my estimate. Independent game from my Dodgers position for diversification. Empty book, so I set my own fair price.

orders.market_title: Will Joey Chestnut eat 82+ hot dogs in the Men's Division of the 2026 Nathan's Famous Fourth of July International Hot Dog Eating Contest?
orders.reasoning: Chestnut is still the overwhelming favorite to win, but 82+ is far beyond his Nathan's contest record of 76 and well above common 2026 betting totals around the low 70s. He ate 70.5 in the 2025 Nathan's event, so I price an 82+ result as a meaningful but fairly remote right-tail outcome.

orders.market_title: Will Miki Sudo eat 56+ hot dogs in the Women's Division of the 2026 Nathan's Famous Fourth of July International Hot Dog Eating Contest?
orders.reasoning: Sudo is dominant, but 56+ would require clearing her women's Nathan's record of 51 by five full hot dogs. After a 33-hot-dog winning total in 2025, I view 56+ as a low-probability outlier even allowing for a rebound toward her best form.

orders.market_title: Golden State vs Atlanta winner?
orders.reasoning: Atlanta is a 3.5-point favorite against Golden State, translating to approximately a 63% win probability. I am placing a limit bid at 60c to capture the value if the market trades down to this level.

orders.market_title: Paraguay vs France Winner?
orders.reasoning: France is heavily favored against Paraguay in the Round of 16. As tournament favorites, their true probability to win in regulation is close to 68%. I am providing liquidity here with a bid at 63 cents.

orders.market_title: Canada vs Morocco Winner?
orders.reasoning: Morocco is on a massive winning streak and is ranked 6th in the world, while Canada is ranked 30th. Even with Canada having somewhat of a geographical edge in Houston, Morocco is the better team. I estimate Morocco's regulation win probability at 58%.

orders.market_title: Will Jannik Sinner win the Sinner vs Struff: Quarterfinal match?
orders.reasoning: Sinner is the clear class edge: current market/odds screens make him an overwhelming favorite against Struff, and their head-to-head is 3-0 in Sinner's favor. Struff's serve gives him some grass-court upset tail, but over best-of-five Sinner's return quality and baseline consistency should dominate. I am bidding below my 94% fair value because the book is currently empty.

orders.market_title: Colorado vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Dodgers are a dominant 59-31 playing at home against a struggling 37-54 Rockies team. With Roki Sasaki projected to pitch for Los Angeles, their true win probability is closer to 70%, making a 65c bid solid value.

orders.market_title: Colorado vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: Dodgers are the top team in the NL and host the Rockies, the worst team in MLB (per 2026 standings). Home Dodgers vs a bottom-tier Colorado club is a genuine ~77% spot. Book is empty, so I'm resting a 74c bid to capture edge over fair value.

orders.market_title: Los Angeles A vs Texas Winner?
orders.reasoning: Texas (45-43) hosts the Angels, who at 36-54 are among the worst teams in the 2026 AL. A solid home team facing a weak road club supports ~64% for the Rangers. Empty book, so resting a 60c bid below fair value for positive EV.

orders.market_title: Will Jannik Sinner win the Sinner vs Struff: Quarterfinal match?
orders.reasoning: Sinner is defending champion/world No. 1 caliber on grass and the external market is pricing him around the mid-90s implied probability (roughly 1/25 vs 16/1). Struff's run is impressive but came with Hurkacz retiring hurt, and this is his first major quarterfinal at age 36. With no Kalshi book showing, I am posting below my 94% fair estimate rather than chasing to the max.

orders.market_title: Will Novak Djokovic win the Auger-Aliassime vs Djokovic: Quarterfinal match?
orders.reasoning: External odds/projection sources make Djokovic roughly a 65-66% favorite over Auger-Aliassime. FAA is seeded higher and serving well, but he just came through a long five-set fourth round, while Djokovic's Wimbledon quarterfinal track record and return quality justify a modest favorite price. I am posting a small order at 62c, a few points below my estimate, because the Kalshi book is currently empty.

orders.market_title: Colorado vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Dodgers are massive favorites (-230 ML) against the Rockies, with Eric Lauer facing Kyle Freeland. The no-vig probability is around 66.5%. I am placing a resting bid at 63c to capture a small edge if filled.

orders.market_title: New York M vs Atlanta Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Braves are favored at home against the Mets (-131 ML). The no-vig probability is roughly 54.9%. Placing a bid at 52c to capture value if liquidity arrives.

orders.market_title: Philadelphia vs Kansas City Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Phillies have Cristopher Sanchez starting against Noah Cameron and are heavy favorites (-210 ML). The no-vig probability for PHI is 68%. Placing a bid at 65c.

orders.market_title: New York Y vs Tampa Bay Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Rays are slight home favorites over the Yankees (-115 ML). Fair probability is 51.1%. Placing a limit order at 49c to supply liquidity below fair value.

orders.market_title: Colorado vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: The Dodgers hosting the Rockies is one of the largest talent mismatches in MLB. Colorado has consistently been the weakest team in the league, especially on the road away from Coors Field, while the Dodgers are a perennial powerhouse at home. I estimate LAD win ~73%; resting a bid at 70c leaves margin.

orders.market_title: Argentina vs Egypt Winner?
orders.reasoning: Argentina, the reigning power and among the world's best sides, is heavily favored over Egypt. This market is regulation-time only (no extra time/penalties), which caps a favorite's win probability due to draw risk. I estimate Argentina reg-time win ~63%; bidding 60c leaves a small edge.

orders.market_title: Houston vs Washington Winner?
orders.reasoning: Houston hosting Washington is a matchup between a strong contender and a rebuilding club. The Astros' rotation and lineup depth give them a clear home-field edge over the Nationals. I estimate HOU win ~64%; resting a bid at 58c provides margin against pitcher-matchup uncertainty.

orders.market_title: Colorado vs Los Angeles D Winner?
orders.reasoning: Dodgers are a materially stronger club and at home against Colorado, with public probable-pitcher info showing Justin Wrobleski in much better form than Michael Lorenzen. I make Los Angeles about 72% to win, broadly consistent with -270-ish market odds but leave a small edge by bidding 70 rather than paying above fair.

notes.text: Week of 2026-06-29: PASSING. Surveyed the in-season slate — MLB game lines (Jul 4-5), the 2026 World Cup knockout matches (Portugal-Croatia, Argentina-Cape Verde, Paraguay-France, USA-Belgium, etc.), WNBA games, MLB/World Cup futures, and World Cup head-to-head markets. Every market I checked returned null bid/ask/last prices with empty orderbooks — there is no live pricing and no resting liquidity anywhere available to me this session. Without a market price I cannot measure a 5-point edge, and without a counterparty a limit order either sits unfilled or fills at an arbitrary self-chosen price with no validation. Per my discipline (bet only with a demonstrable edge; respect liquidity; never force action), the correct decision is to deploy zero capital and preserve the full 100.00 bankroll and my Brier calibration for a week where tradeable prices actually exist. No bets placed.

Passed this week. I surveyed MLB game/total/RFI markets, WNBA games/futures, Wimbledon tennis match markets, Nathan's hot dog contest, F1, and NFL/MLB futures, but the candidate markets I checked showed no visible bid/ask and empty order books. With no reliable market price or available liquidity, I cannot establish a 5+ percentage point edge or size responsibly, so I am preserving bankroll.

Attempted to place 5 limit orders this week on MLB and World Cup markets. I targeted Cleveland (vs CWS), Dodgers (vs Padres), and Mariners (vs Angels) in MLB, alongside Portugal (vs Croatia) and Switzerland (vs Algeria) in World Cup matches, projecting 5-7% edges on all. Sized conservatively via fractional Kelly. Unfortunately, the exchange rejected all orders due to an underlying API endpoint deprecation (410: deprecated_v1_order_endpoint). Passing this week as I am unable to successfully execute orders due to this technical constraint.

Week of 2026-06-29 — Intended to bet but BLOCKED BY A PLATFORM BUG. Every place_bet call was rejected by the exchange with: 400 'json: cannot unmarshal number into Go struct field CreateOrderV2Request.count of type string' — a server-side serialization defect (count expected as string, sent as number). This hit all markets/sizes/sides; not a risk-limit or input issue on my end (5 attempts made).

Strategy I would have executed (World Cup 2026 knockout, regulation-time moneylines, all books empty so I was supplying liquidity below fair value):
1) Argentina to beat Cape Verde in regulation — fair ~75% (draw ~16%, upset ~8%); wanted 6 contracts YES @ 70c.
2) France to beat Paraguay in regulation — fair ~63% (Paraguay can grind a 90-min draw); wanted 4 contracts YES @ 58c.
Both sized at roughly quarter-Kelly with a cushion under fair value. Passing this week is forced entirely by the exchange rejecting orders, which is the documented zero-execution exception.

Week of 2026-06-29 session. Surveyed the board: MLB and WNBA are the live daily markets. Books are currently empty (null prices) on the games I inspected, which is fine — I was prepared to supply liquidity with a resting limit at my own fair estimate. My single most defensible edge: San Francisco over Colorado (Rockies are MLB's weakest team; SF a modest ~57% favorite even discounting for Coors and unknown pitchers this far out), and I intended a small, sub-quarter-Kelly resting buy at 50-52c. HOWEVER, every order attempt was rejected by an exchange-side serialization bug: the API returns 400 "cannot unmarshal number into Go struct field CreateOrderV2Request.count of type string" — it expects the contract count as a string but receives a number. I confirmed this is NOT a risk-limit, liquidity, or cash issue by retrying across multiple markets (MLB SF/COL, WNBA DAL/CONN), multiple quantities (4, 5, 6, 10), and multiple prices (50, 52). The rejection is deterministic and outside my control. Documenting the intended bet (SF vs COL YES, ~6 contracts, 52c, p=0.57) for transparency; no position could be opened due to the platform error.

Surveyed open sports markets and found mostly empty books. My preferred position was a small YES on Miki Sudo to win the 2026 Nathan's women's hot dog contest at 70c (true estimate 80%), with Joey Chestnut and Las Vegas WNBA also considered as backup liquidity-providing orders. Multiple place_bet attempts were rejected by the exchange/API with a serialization error (`count` expected as string), so no order appears to have been accepted despite attempting to satisfy the mandatory-bet requirement.

I re-checked the board and found many open sports markets but mostly empty order books. I attempted multiple one-contract conservative bets (Miki Sudo YES, Joey Chestnut YES, Las Vegas WNBA YES), but every valid order was rejected by an exchange/tool serialization error: the API is sending contract count as a number where the exchange expects a string. No bet was successfully accepted despite repeated attempts.

Found multiple edges in MLB and WNBA matches where orderbooks were empty. Determined fair probabilities (Dodgers 58%, Yankees 59%, Orioles 56%, Sun 65%) and attempted to provide liquidity using limit orders priced at a 4% edge (Dodgers at 54c, Yankees at 55c, Orioles at 52c, Sun at 61c), sizing each bet to ~0.25 Kelly (4 contracts each). Unfortunately, all place_bet calls were rejected by the exchange API with a type unmarshaling error ('cannot unmarshal number into Go struct field CreateOrderV2Request.count of type string'). Despite the technical failure, the strategy remains to provide liquidity at fair value minus an edge when books are empty.

I attempted to place bets on Portugal to beat Croatia (KXWCGAME-26JUL02PORCRO-POR) at 47c (5 contracts), Dodgers to beat Padres (KXMLBGAME-26JUL042210SDLAD-LAD) at 55c (5 contracts), and Connecticut to beat Dallas (KXWNBAGAME-26JUL02DALCONN-CONN) at 63c (5 contracts). However, all place_bet tool calls failed because the backend proxy is sending the 'contracts' count as an integer instead of a string to the Kalshi API, resulting in a '400 Bad Request: cannot unmarshal number into Go struct field CreateOrderV2Request.count of type string'. I am unable to execute any bets due to this platform error.

Placed one small resting limit order: 5 YES on Jannik Sinner to beat Jenson Brooksby in the Wimbledon R32 match at up to 90c, with a 93% forecast. Most surveyed MLB/WNBA/F1/tennis markets had empty books, so I supplied liquidity at a price consistent with my estimate rather than forcing larger exposure; sizing stayed small given no live injury/news feed.

For the week of 2026-06-29, all evaluated markets (MLB, WNBA, World Cup) featured empty order books. I capitalized on this by acting as a market maker, setting limit orders representing fair probabilities derived from underlying team strength minus a 5% edge margin. I deployed near the maximum allowed limit of $50 across 5 unique games: backed Las Vegas Aces and New York Liberty in WNBA action based on their elite star power, took the Yankees and Dodgers in MLB due to their deep lineups and favorable matchups, and took Portugal in regulation over Croatia in the World Cup given Croatia's aging squad. By keeping my bids 5 percentage points below my true probability estimates, these orders will provide positive expected value if they are filled by market participants.

Week of 2026-06-29: placed one small liquidity-supplying order on Joey Chestnut YES at 93c for the 2026 Nathan's men’s winner market. With no Kalshi book showing, I used external odds and historical dominance to mark Chestnut around 94.5%, but kept size small (5 contracts) because the edge over a fair favorite price is modest and one-off contest variance/disqualification risk remains.

I have already hit the weekly hard limit of entering 5 new markets this week (new_positions_remaining_this_week is 0). Since all my prior positions have already closed or settled (open_positions is empty), there are no existing markets I can add to. Therefore, I am physically blocked by the risk limits from placing any further bets this session. I identified edges on the Pirates (vs Nationals) and Dodgers (vs Padres) based on starting pitcher mismatches, but the system rejected the orders. Wrapping up the session.

I have exactly 0 open positions and 0 new positions remaining this week according to the bankroll limits. Therefore, every single bet I attempt to place is rejected by the system's hard cap. I have attempted to place bets multiple times to satisfy the session requirements, but they are hard-rejected. There are no available markets that I am permitted to enter.

Weekly strategy: I used the currently empty order books to supply liquidity on Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest tail outcomes rather than chase thin game markets. I bought NO on Joey Chestnut 82+ at 84c and NO on Miki Sudo 56+ at 90c, small sizes under the 10% per-market cap; both are priced as extreme right-tail totals relative to their Nathan's records and recent 2025 results.

This week, I focused on high-profile sporting events taking place on July 4, 2026. Given empty order books on Kalshi for these events, I established my own fair market bids to supply liquidity. 

First, I placed bids on France to defeat Paraguay in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 matchup, estimating France's regulation win probability at 68% and placing a resting bid at 63c. Second, I bet on Morocco to win against Canada, estimating Morocco's win probability at 58% due to their higher ranking and 33-game win streak, with a resting bid at 53c. Lastly, in the WNBA, I targeted the Atlanta Dream over the Golden State Valkyries, sizing the bid based on the Dream being a 3.5-point favorite which implies a 63% win probability. I placed a resting bid at 60c. My strategy relies on setting honest bids that reflect genuine probabilities to capture long-term edge.

Weekly 2026-07-06 strategy: surveyed MLB, WNBA, ATP and WTA match markets; most available books were empty, so I supplied liquidity rather than forcing thin unknowns. Main position is 6 YES contracts on Jannik Sinner to beat Jan-Lennard Struff at 92c, with a 94% calibrated forecast; price is below my fair value and supported by Sinner's No. 1-level form, 3-0 H2H edge, and best-of-five favorite profile. Kept size modest because it is a very high-price tennis position with upset/injury tail risk.

Found a strong mismatch in the July 8 MLB game between the Dodgers and the Rockies. The Dodgers are 59-31 playing at home against the 37-54 Rockies, with Roki Sasaki projected to start. The orderbook was empty, so I placed a resting limit order at 65c to buy YES on LAD, estimating their true win probability at 70%. Kept sizing moderate (10 contracts) to respect the Kelly fraction.

Weekly strategy 2026-07-06: focused on Wimbledon ATP markets because Kalshi books were empty but external prices and matchup context gave usable fair values. Posted small, price-disciplined resting YES orders: Sinner over Struff (6 @ 92c, p=94%) and Djokovic over Auger-Aliassime (3 @ 62c, p=65.5%). Kept sizing modest with no current Kalshi liquidity and avoided forcing oversized positions.

This week, I surveyed the MLB slate for July 6th. The orderbooks were empty for these games, so I placed resting limit orders (bids) for 4 heavy or slight favorites just below their no-vig probabilities derived from sportsbooks moneyline odds.

- LAD (vs COL) bid at 63c (Forecast: 66.5%)
- ATL (vs NYM) bid at 52c (Forecast: 54.9%)
- PHI (vs KC) bid at 65c (Forecast: 68.0%)
- TB (vs NYY) bid at 49c (Forecast: 51.1%)

These positions represent small fractional Kelly bets (~10 contracts each) to ensure diversification while adhering to the risk limits.

Week of 2026-07-06: All surveyed markets (MLB games, World Cup knockout games) had empty orderbooks, so I supplied liquidity with small resting limit orders priced below my honest fair-value estimates on defensible favorites. (1) Dodgers vs Rockies — biggest talent gap in MLB, LAD YES @70c (est 73%). (2) Argentina vs Egypt reg-time moneyline — clear class gap but capped by draw risk in 90 min, ARG YES @60c (est 63%). (3) Astros vs Nationals — contender at home vs rebuilder, HOU YES @58c (est 64%). Three independent, small positions (~6% of bankroll total) using quarter-Kelly sizing scaled down for pitcher-matchup uncertainty. Unfilled portions refund at settlement.

Weekly strategy: surveyed open MLB/WNBA markets and focused on the clearest near-term edge with interpretable information. Placed a small, disciplined 2-contract YES bid at 70c on Dodgers to beat Rockies, estimating true win probability at 72% based on home-field strength and a favorable listed pitching matchup; size kept well below Kelly because the book was empty and the edge is modest.

This week, I surveyed the MLB slate for July 8-9. Found empty orderbooks across the board, providing an opportunity to post resting limit orders at prices below fair value (derived from current sportsbook odds). I placed 10 contracts on San Diego (-160 ML, 61% win prob) at 55c, 10 contracts on Milwaukee (-128 ML, 56% win prob) at 51c, and 5 contracts on Tampa Bay (~54% win prob) at 49c. Each order targets roughly a 4-6% edge over consensus probabilities.

I scanned upcoming WNBA (Indiana vs LA), World Cup (France vs Morocco), and MLB games. While I found relevant injury news (e.g., Aliyah Boston out for Indiana Fever), there were no resting bids or asks in any of the corresponding Kalshi markets. With completely empty orderbooks, I cannot accurately gauge market prices or secure an immediate fill with a verifiable edge. In accordance with strict bankroll discipline and risk management, I am sitting this session out to wait for markets with real liquidity.

2026-07-08: Passing today. Surveyed the imminent slate — WNBA games tonight (IND/LA, MIN/CONN, GS/TOR) and the next MLB games (July 10-11). Every market checked returned null bid/ask, zero volume, and an empty orderbook. With no quoted prices and no resting liquidity, there is no mispricing to measure against and no realistic fill available, so no bet can clear the 3c-after-fee edge bar or actually execute. Protecting bankroll and waiting for a day with live, tradable prices. No action taken.

Passed this session: the listed July 8 MLB/WNBA markets had no actionable bid/ask, last price, volume, or open interest in the exchange feed, so there was no realistic fill/liquidity to evaluate against fresh news. I’ll preserve bankroll and wait for priced, liquid day-of markets.
